case.title: Dead Heat
case.subtitle: A murder at the rail
case.setting: Race night at a Louisiana horse track. Jockey Julian Cross is found dead near the service corridor between the paddock and stable lane. Officials call it a horse accident. The evidence says otherwise.

finalQuestions.label: Why was Julian killed?
options: A rival jockey wanted him out of the way
He was going to expose that Midnight Halo was unfit to race and tied to betting manipulation
He owed money to the wrong people
He discovered stolen tack and stable supplies

finalQuestions.label: How was the murder carried out?
options: He slipped from the paddock rail and struck his head
He was killed instantly by a horse kick in the lane
Loose racing gear caused a fatal crush injury
Julian was attacked in the service/tack corridor and the scene was staged as a horse accident

finalQuestions.label: What is the strongest proof clue?
options: The pre-impact incapacitation finding combined with the horse condition evidence
Fresh wrap fibers in the stall

suspects.name: Marlon Price
suspects.public_read: Experienced, controlled, and defensive. Trainer of Midnight Halo. Had the most to lose if the horse was scratched.

suspects.name: Rafael Morrow
suspects.public_read: Intense and openly hostile toward Julian. Their public argument makes Rafael look dangerous immediately.

suspects.name: Diane Voss
suspects.public_read: Precise, clinical, and withholding. Knows more about Midnight Halo’s real condition than she first admits.

suspects.name: Tommy Brigg
suspects.public_read: Smooth and well-connected. Tracks odds movement and knows exactly how much money was riding on Midnight Halo.

suspects.name: Leah Sloane
suspects.public_read: Wealthy, polished, and impatient. Pressured multiple people to keep the horse active.

suspects.name: Eddie Vale
suspects.public_read: Nervous and inconsistent. Saw movement in restricted areas and changed parts of his story.

crushed_straw_pattern.title: Crushed Straw Pattern
crushed_straw_pattern.description: The straw beneath Julian's body was compressed differently from the wider disturbance nearby, suggesting placement after the initial struggle.

tack_chest_corner_blood.title: Tack Chest Corner Blood
tack_chest_corner_blood.description: A small blood transfer on the corner of a tack chest suggests Julian first struck something indoors before ending up near the lane.

broken_stirrup_leather.title: Broken Stirrup Leather
broken_stirrup_leather.description: The leather was cut under tension rather than snapped in panic, weakening the idea of a chaotic horse accident.

stable_wall_scuff_mark.title: Stable Wall Scuff Mark
stable_wall_scuff_mark.description: A shoulder-height scuff shows Julian contacted the wall before going down.

clean_boot_sole.title: Clean Boot Sole
clean_boot_sole.description: Julian's boot sole lacks the mud-straw mix expected if he walked through the lane as claimed.

midnight_halo_tendon_swelling.title: Midnight Halo Tendon Swelling
midnight_halo_tendon_swelling.description: Fresh foreleg swelling indicates Midnight Halo should have been scratched from the race.

pain_masking_injection_site.title: Pain Masking Injection Site
pain_masking_injection_site.description: A recent injection mark suggests treatment intended to hide pain rather than address a resolved issue.

uneven_sweat_pattern.title: Uneven Sweat Pattern
uneven_sweat_pattern.description: The horse's sweat pattern indicates limited controlled movement, not a full prep routine.

fresh_wrap_fibers.title: Fresh Wrap Fibers
fresh_wrap_fibers.description: Loose wrap fibers in the stall indicate a late rewrapping that conflicts with the official prep timeline.

silver_regent_prep_log.title: Silver Regent Prep Log
silver_regent_prep_log.description: Silver Regent's prep log is complete and normal, highlighting the suspicious gaps around Midnight Halo.

vet_clearance_addendum.title: Vet Clearance Addendum
vet_clearance_addendum.description: A handwritten addendum softened the original veterinary concern about Midnight Halo's condition.

preimpact_incapacitation.title: Pre-Impact Incapacitation Finding
preimpact_incapacitation.description: Medical review indicates Julian was already compromised before the final fatal impact.

injury_angle_report.title: Injury Angle Report
injury_angle_report.description: The fatal injury angle does not match the described horse kick or accidental trampling scenario.

sedation_trace_summary.title: Sedation Trace Summary
sedation_trace_summary.description: Trace substances point to recent treatment pressure around the horse, increasing motive to silence Julian.

tack_room_signout_sheet.title: Tack Room Sign-Out Sheet
tack_room_signout_sheet.description: A tack item was logged twice under conflicting times, suggesting timeline manipulation.

service_corridor_camera_gap.title: Service Corridor Camera Gap
service_corridor_camera_gap.description: A blind spot between paddock access and the tack corridor created an ideal hidden movement route.

stall_mucking_log.title: Stall Mucking Log
stall_mucking_log.description: A stall cleaning time conflicts with a rider's account of who was present and when.

paddock_gate_latch_mark.title: Paddock Gate Latch Mark
paddock_gate_latch_mark.description: Fresh latch wear suggests hurried movement through a restricted access point.

bandage_disposal_bin.title: Bandage Disposal Bin
bandage_disposal_bin.description: Used wraps were discarded later than Marlon claimed the horse had been cleared.

rafaels_threat_statement.title: Rafael's Threat Statement
rafaels_threat_statement.description: Multiple witnesses heard Rafael threaten Julian after their public argument.

eddies_revised_timeline.title: Eddie's Revised Timeline
eddies_revised_timeline.description: Eddie changed his statement after first denying he saw Marlon in the service corridor.

dianes_delayed_admission.title: Diane's Delayed Admission
dianes_delayed_admission.description: Diane finally admits Midnight Halo's condition was worse than initially reported.

tommys_odds_call_note.title: Tommy's Odds Call Note
tommys_odds_call_note.description: A betting note shows concern over a late scratch affecting major money.

leahs_pressure_text.title: Leah's Pressure Text
leahs_pressure_text.description: A text message pushes to keep Midnight Halo active no matter what.

julians_pocket_note.title: Julian's Pocket Note
julians_pocket_note.description: Julian wrote down 'Halo,' 'clearance,' and 'money' before his death.

public.text: Final stable prep begins for the feature race.
Midnight Halo is rewrapped in the stall area.
Diane Voss notes concern about Midnight Halo's foreleg.
Julian Cross argues quietly with Marlon Price near the tack room.
Rafael Morrow has a public confrontation with Julian near paddock entry.
Tommy Brigg places a call about odds movement on the feature race.
Leah Sloane sends pressure text insisting the horse stays active.
Eddie Vale reports seeing movement through the service corridor.
Julian is last seen heading toward the tack/service corridor.
Julian is found near the stable lane, with the scene initially treated as a horse accident.

hints.1: Focus on physical contradictions first. The scene itself weakens the accident theory before motive does.
hints.2: Track who could move through the service corridor without being clearly seen.
hints.3: Use Midnight Halo's condition to test whether witness statements are believable.
hints.4: Not every lie is about murder. Some lies protect money, pressure, or reputations.
hints.5: The killer needed access, motive, and knowledge of how to fake a horse-related accident.

marlon_price.name: Marlon Price
marlon_price.intro: Marlon folds a saddle cloth over one arm and barely looks at you. 'Julian liked to make noise when he thought he'd found something. Doesn't mean he was right.'

topics.label: Julian's accusation
topics.response: Julian said Midnight Halo should be scratched. I told him he was out of line and out of his lane.

topics.label: Midnight Halo's condition
topics.response: The horse was tight, not lame. There's a difference. People love to call a horse unfit when they don't understand prep.

topics.label: Service corridor movement
topics.response: I was in the paddock sequence, not wandering service halls. Ask the stable crew if you're lost.

topics.label: Why were the wraps changed?
topics.response: Because prep changed. Horses get adjusted. That is not a crime unless you need it to be one.

rafael_morrow.name: Rafael Morrow
rafael_morrow.intro: Rafael leans back with his jaw tight. 'Yeah, I argued with him. Half this track argued with him. That doesn't make me your killer.'

topics.label: Your argument with Julian
topics.response: He liked sticking his nose into everybody's ride. I told him to worry about his own horse.

topics.label: Last time you saw Julian
topics.response: Near paddock entry. He was heated, distracted, and looking for somebody.

topics.label: Bayou Ember's prep timing
topics.response: My horse was being settled, not held up in service lanes. You can check the tack sheet.

topics.label: Did you threaten him?
topics.response: I said things. Everybody heard me. That's different from following through.

diane_voss.name: Diane Voss
diane_voss.intro: Diane removes one glove finger by finger before speaking. 'If you want truth, ask precise questions. I'm not here to clean up everyone else's panic.'

topics.label: Midnight Halo's exam
topics.response: There was inflammation. Whether it rose to scratch level depends on pressure, judgment, and who is pretending not to hear me.

topics.label: Clearance paperwork
topics.response: The original note was cautious. The addendum softened it. I did not create that pressure in a vacuum.

topics.label: Could the horse have caused this?
topics.response: Not in the clean, simple way people are describing. The injury pattern does not fit the story being sold.

topics.label: Medication use
topics.response: There are signs of recent treatment. Whether it was therapeutic or deceptive is the more useful question.

tommy_brigg.name: Tommy Brigg
tommy_brigg.intro: Tommy smiles like you're both in on a joke. 'People get real interested in odds after something bad happens. Funny how that works.'

topics.label: Odds movement
topics.response: When a favorite might scratch, phones ring. That's not sinister. That's racing.

topics.label: Your contact with stable staff
topics.response: I talk to people. That doesn't mean I walk horses, wrap legs, or kill jockeys.

topics.label: Midnight Halo staying active
topics.response: Plenty of people needed that horse in the race. I wasn't the only one with money sweating.

topics.label: Julian as a threat
topics.response: He was becoming a problem because he was loud. Loud gets expensive.

leah_sloane.name: Leah Sloane
leah_sloane.intro: Leah does not sit. 'If this is about race operations, speak clearly. I do not enjoy being dragged into stable gossip.'

topics.label: Your stake in the race
topics.response: I had expectations, yes. That is not the same as desperation.

topics.label: Pressure to keep the horse running
topics.response: I expected professionalism. If someone interpreted that as pressure, that reflects on them.

topics.label: Your relationship with Marlon
topics.response: I fund outcomes. He manages horses. Do not confuse influence with control.

topics.label: Julian's threat to expose things
topics.response: He was emotional and self-important. Men like that mistake attention for leverage.

eddie_vale.name: Eddie Vale
eddie_vale.intro: Eddie keeps glancing toward the barn doors. 'I didn't do nothing. I just saw people where they shouldn't have been, that's all.'

topics.label: What did you see in the corridor?
topics.response: I saw movement where there shouldn't have been movement. I didn't want to be part of it.

topics.label: Why did your timeline change?
topics.response: Because the first time I answered, I was scared. The second time, I was still scared.

topics.label: Midnight Halo's wraps
topics.response: They got changed late. Later than they should've, if you ask me.

topics.label: Did you see Marlon with Julian?
topics.response: Not face-to-face the whole time. But I saw Marlon go where he said he never went.

scene_001_body_position.title: Body Position
scene_001_body_position.text: Julian Cross lies near the stable lane, just far enough from the tack corridor to support an accident story. But the straw pattern and surrounding damage do not line up cleanly.

scene_002_tack_area.title: Tack Area
scene_002_tack_area.text: The tack corridor shows signs of a more controlled struggle than the open lane outside. A tack chest corner bears a small blood transfer.

scene_003_public_conflict.title: Public Conflict
scene_003_public_conflict.text: Witnesses confirm Julian argued publicly with Rafael, but physical evidence suggests the death scene cannot be explained by that confrontation alone.

scene_004_checkpoint.prompt: What most strongly suggests Julian's death scene was staged?
answers: The body was found near the stable lane
Blood transfer and impact signs point to an earlier indoor struggle
scene_004_checkpoint.correct: The strongest early contradiction is physical. The tack chest blood and wall scuff suggest Julian was first injured in the corridor area before being placed near the lane.
scene_004_checkpoint.incorrect: Focus on the physical evidence first. The argument is suspicious, but it does not explain the scene mechanics.

scene_005_rider_accounts.title: Rider Accounts
scene_005_rider_accounts.text: The jockeys agree on very little, but several statements cannot all be true at once.

scene_006_stable_logs.title: Stable Logs
scene_006_stable_logs.text: Routine logs show inconsistencies around cleaning, tack use, and who could have been in the corridor at the critical window.

scene_007_checkpoint.prompt: Which new clue most weakens the witness timeline?
answers: A crowd argument in the paddock
scene_007_checkpoint.correct: The camera gap matters because it creates a hidden movement route. Once you know that blind spot exists, multiple witness statements become less reliable.
scene_007_checkpoint.incorrect: Look for the clue that changes how people could move unseen, not just what motivated them.

scene_008_horse_condition.title: Horse Condition
scene_008_horse_condition.text: Midnight Halo's physical state becomes the central challenge to the official version of events.

scene_009_prep_records.title: Prep Records
scene_009_prep_records.text: Compared against other horses, Midnight Halo's prep timeline is full of irregularities.

scene_010_checkpoint.prompt: What horse evidence most undermines the official accident story?
answers: Midnight Halo was a crowd favorite
Midnight Halo showed fresh tendon swelling and should not have been fully active
Silver Regent had a normal prep log
scene_010_checkpoint.correct: If Midnight Halo was not fit for normal prep and handling, then the story built around stable chaos and horse reaction becomes much weaker.
scene_010_checkpoint.incorrect: Choose the clue that physically limits what the horse could have been doing.

scene_011_money_pressure.title: Money Pressure
scene_011_money_pressure.text: Betting pressure, owner influence, and hidden financial risk begin to explain why so many people lied.

scene_012_late_wraps.title: Late Wraps
scene_012_late_wraps.text: Discarded wraps and timeline slippage show Midnight Halo's condition was being managed far later than Marlon claimed.

scene_013_checkpoint.prompt: What is the strongest motive pattern emerging?
answers: Personal revenge over a past loss
A betting-linked effort to keep an unfit horse in the race
scene_013_checkpoint.correct: By this point, the evidence shows pressure from money and race outcome manipulation, not just personal rivalry.
scene_013_checkpoint.incorrect: Think bigger than public conflict. The motive is tied to risk, money, and exposure.

scene_014_medical_reconstruction.title: Medical Reconstruction
scene_014_medical_reconstruction.text: The final medical findings destroy the horse accident narrative and point to deliberate staging.

scene_015_route_reconstruction.title: Route Reconstruction
scene_015_route_reconstruction.text: The service corridor blind spot, the false body placement, and Marlon's timeline contradictions now align into one coherent sequence.

scene_016_checkpoint.prompt: What proves Julian was not killed in a true horse accident?
answers: Julian was incapacitated before the final impact and the injury pattern does not match the claimed accident
scene_016_checkpoint.correct: The medical findings and impact geometry prove the stable accident explanation is false.
scene_016_checkpoint.incorrect: Look for the combination of evidence that defeats the official cause of death, not just suspicious behavior.

scene_016_final.title: Final Accusation
scene_016_final.text: You have enough to decide who killed Julian Cross, why they did it, how the death was staged, and what evidence destroys the accident story.
